home.heading: Hi, I'm Tyler
content: I'm a frontend engineer and engineering leader. At Best Egg, I lead a team shipping customer-facing products that touch millions of users. I've also built for Vanguard, Agilent Technologies, Bank of America, and others.

When I'm not coding, I'm usually behind a drum kit. I've toured nationally with a major-label band on Wind-up Records/Sony BMG, played thousands of gigs, and now take on remote studio sessions and post clips at tyleringersolldrums.com.

home.heading: Engineering
content: I build frontend applications with frameworks like Angular and Vue.js, and lead the teams that ship them. My work spans design systems, authentication, payment processing, and customer-facing products at scale.
View my full resume →

home.heading: Music
content: I've been playing drums since I was a kid. With The Crash Motive (Wind-up Records/Sony BMG), I landed placements in the Super Bowl, Madden NFL, and The Fantastic Four. I also co-founded SpeakerCity, one of the most in-demand event bands in the Mid-Atlantic.
More about my music →

home.heading: Beyond work
content: I'm a father of two, an avid cyclist and runner, a craft beer enthusiast, and a vinyl record collector.
I'm based in Kennett Square, Pennsylvania.

resume.heading: Resume
content: I'm a frontend engineer and engineering leader with a track record of shipping production applications at scale. I've built for Best Egg, Vanguard, Bank of America, Agilent Technologies, DuPont, and Chemours across fintech, financial services, scientific instrumentation, and enterprise software.

roles.subheading: Director of Software Engineering II · 2026 - Present
content: As Director of Software Engineering II, I lead a team of engineers across Best Egg's customer-facing identity verification, payment servicing, and authentication applications, products that touch millions of customer interactions annually. Drive technical planning and roadmap alignment with stakeholders, product, designers, and UX.
Engineering areas:
• Customer verifications: identity verification and fraud prevention
• Payment servicing: payment processing, auto pay, payment methods, due date changes, flexible relief programs
• Authentication: login, signup, password recovery, customer servicing hub

• Lead third-party service integrations across customer support, customer data and analytics, income verification, and direct debt payoff, owning vendor evaluation, technical integration, and production delivery
• Champion team adoption of AI-assisted development tooling with Cursor AI and GitHub Copilot to accelerate engineering velocity and code quality
Tech: Angular, TypeScript, Jasmine, GitHub Actions, Node.js, Nginx, Django, Python, DataDog, FullStory

As Senior Lead Engineer II, I owned frontend engineering across Best Egg's verification, payment servicing, and authentication applications. Drove roadmap planning and technical direction alongside product, stakeholders, and UX, and mentored engineers on the team.
Engineering areas:
• Customer verifications: identity verification and fraud prevention
• Payment servicing: payment processing, auto pay, payment methods, due date changes, flexible relief programs
• Authentication: login, signup, password recovery, customer servicing hub
• Implemented Content Security Policy (CSP) with nonce-based script integration across customer-facing applications, strengthening frontend security posture
Tech: Angular, TypeScript, Jasmine, GitHub Actions, Node.js, Nginx, Django, Python, DataDog, FullStory

Came into Best Egg to modernize a legacy Angular codebase and built the foundation the team works from today. Overhauled the architecture, migrated the design system, and established the patterns and tooling that stuck.
• Refactored Angular application architecture for lazy loading modules, reduced main build bundle size
• Migrated Bootstrap to Angular Material, upgraded Angular multiple versions to current
• Established design system with Storybook and Tailwind CSS, created npm library for brand styles and shared components
• Built no-auth quick pay and auto payment scheduling features
• Golden Egg Award for "BE a Builder of Great Teams" (Q3 2023)
Tech: Angular, TypeScript, Angular Material, Storybook, Tailwind CSS, Jasmine, GitHub Actions, Docker, Nginx, Node.js
As the lead frontend engineer for Vanguard's Financial Advisor Services, I designed and built a design system supporting thousands of advisors and millions of their clients across the country.
• Drove technical implementation of FAS design system through division-wide rebrand

• Architected web component solution with Angular-compatible custom element wrapper, enabling cross-framework integration (Vue, Angular, React, Adobe Experience Manager) and eliminating framework-specific support teams
• Architected and developed Vue.js/NestJS application prototype that became the standard foundation for all FAS single-page applications
• Presented integration strategy to IT leadership and drove adoption across three Angular development teams
• Implemented CI/CD deployment infrastructure for internal documentation using AWS services
Tech: Vue.js, NestJS, Web Components, Storybook, Angular, Custom Elements, AWS, CI/CD

Embedded with Agilent Technologies's R&D software team as the sole frontend specialist, building web-based instrument control interfaces shipped globally with Agilent's flagship hardware product lines.
• Developed web-based instrument control UI for Agilent's flagship Gas Chromatography hardware using Sencha/Ext JS, implementing real-time monitoring and control through WebSocket connections
• Created data visualization features including live instrument data graphing and visual hardware configuration displays
• Designed custom SVG icons and CSS styling aligned with Agilent's product design standards

• Architected automated build system for Adobe RoboHelp documentation delivery across Gas Chromatography, Mass Spectrometry, and Sampler product lines, supporting 8+ languages with dynamic template generation, theming, and command-line automation for multi-language localization
Tech: JavaScript, Sencha/Ext JS, HTML5, CSS/SCSS, SVG, WebSockets, Adobe RoboHelp, Command-line scripting

As a frontend specialist embedded across multiple concurrent engagements, I shipped web, email, and enterprise solutions for Fortune 500 brands including Bank of America, David's Bridal, Chemours, and DuPont, reaching tens of millions of users.
Frontend Development:
• Developed responsive websites and landing pages for enterprise clients like Bank of America, David's Bridal, Chemours, and Blue Cross Blue Shield Global, reaching millions of users
• Established frontend development standards and best practices across the digital team
Email Development:

• Architected and developed "adaptive" HTML email campaigns for Bank of America's Loyalty, Cash Rewards, and Travel Rewards programs, reaching millions of customers with full cross-platform compatibility
• Led technical strategy for email development, optimizing for deliverability, accessibility, and cross-client consistency
Systems Management:
• Served as Systems Owner for DuPont's SharePoint security auditing system, delivering weekly stakeholder reports and managing server migration with IBM consultants
SharePoint Development:
• Developed SharePoint intranet solutions for Chemours and Everything But Water
• Coordinated time-sensitive deployments of highly sensitive internal communications with Chemours' communications leadership, requiring off-hours execution
Tech: HTML5, CSS/SCSS, JavaScript, jQuery, SharePoint, Responsive Design, Email Development

I co-founded SpeakerCity and built one of the most in-demand event bands in the Mid-Atlantic. We debuted to a crowd of 2,000+ at the University of Delaware's Bob Carpenter Center and immediately landed a summer residency at NorthBeach in Dewey Beach.
• Performed as drummer and DJ at weddings, corporate events, and club venues throughout the Mid-Atlantic
• Composed and produced musical sequences with click tracks, serving as the production backbone for live performances
• Handled business tax filings as part of shared co-founder responsibilities
As a long-term embedded consultant with Agilent Technologies's R&D team, I delivered frontend applications and technical documentation for global scientific instrumentation product lines.
• Designed standalone web-based document delivery applications distributed via USB/DVD for global product lines

• Architected custom HTML templates and automated build systems for Adobe RoboHelp, streamlining multi-product documentation delivery across Gas Chromatography, Mass Spectrometry, and Sampler product lines
• Authored technical documentation using Adobe FrameMaker and converted to web-ready HTML
• Created software UI icons, technical diagrams, and visual assets for R&D teams
Tech: HTML, CSS, JavaScript, Adobe RoboHelp, Adobe FrameMaker, Adobe Illustrator, Adobe Photoshop
Early in my career, I consulted with Agilent Technologies and pharmaceutical clients on technical documentation and web development, then stepped away to tour nationally with The Crash Motive.
• Produced regulatory documentation and compliance materials for pharmaceutical clients
• Developed and maintained corporate web presence
Tech: HTML, CSS, JavaScript, Adobe RoboHelp, Adobe FrameMaker

I co-founded and drummed for The Crash Motive, growing the band from an independent act to a major label deal with Wind-up Records and Sony BMG covering recording, distribution, touring, merchandising, and publishing. Music from the band reached Super Bowl audiences, EA Sports games, major motion pictures, 30+ professional sports stadiums, and primetime television.
• Performed drum parts on studio recordings and arranged drum compositions for albums and singles
• Toured nationally and performed extensively at major venues, opening for nationally recognized acts
• Performed at high-profile industry showcases with globally recognized artists and music executives
• Developed and executed targeted digital marketing campaigns using location and interest-based audience targeting to drive tour attendance and radio engagement in key markets
• Secured high-profile music placements including Super Bowl XLII (Fox), Madden NFL 2007 (EA Sports), and The Fantastic Four movie/soundtrack (20th Century Fox)
• Licensed music for use in 30+ professional sports stadiums (NFL, MLB, NCAA, NHL)
• Placed music in network television programming including NBC, FOX, and CBS promotional campaigns (2006-2008), 2008 Olympic Trials (NBC), NFL Live, NFL Rewind, and Baseball Tonight (ESPN)
• Negotiated and managed business partnerships with major label executives, booking agents, and licensing representatives
Started as an intern and earned the full-time IT Division Intranet Webmaster role, designing and building internal web applications used in production across the division.
• Redesigned and developed the IT Service & Support Division intranet website in Flash
• Designed and developed the Online Portfolio Trust Management Group intranet site
• Designed and developed a custom call logging front-end application with Microsoft Access, Visual Basic, and SQL Server, used in production across multiple service and support departments
• Provided first-level internal tech support for software, network accounts, and hardware
Tech: HTML, CSS, JavaScript, Flash, Microsoft Access, Visual Basic, SQL Server
While finishing my undergraduate degree at the University of Delaware, I supported the Online Portfolio Group on a production Microsoft Access call logging application and web development projects.
Tech: HTML, CSS, JavaScript, Microsoft Access

music.heading: Music
content: I've been playing drums since I was a kid, from the club circuit in Newark, DE to major-label studios and national tours. Below is a look at the projects I've been part of. Covers, studio sessions, and instructional content coming soon at tyleringersolldrums.com.

music.heading: Skitzo Calypso
content: My first serious studio work came through Skitzo Calypso, an alt-hard rock project led by Brad Cox out of Bel Air, Maryland, with Dave Pace producing at 12th Planet Recording Studio. Brad wrote everything and handled vocals and guitar. I played drums and bass across two full albums, Join the Cult (1998) and Premeditated Acts of Stupidity (1999), and landed a track on Electrophoria/P.L.A.A.G.E.S. as well. It was my first real experience recording to a part rather than playing live. The band is still active today:

<iframe allow='autoplay *; encrypted-media *;' frameborder='0' height='150' style='width:100%;max-width:660px;overflow:hidden;background:transparent;' sandbox='allow-forms allow-popups allow-same-origin allow-scripts allow-storage-access-by-user-activation allow-top-navigation-by-user-activation' src='https://embed.music.apple.com/us/album/asphyxiation/1748465468?i=1748465472'></iframe>

music.heading: The Healthy Doses
content: At the same time, I was at the University of Delaware playing in a jam band. The Healthy Doses were a big part of the Newark, DE scene in the late 90s. Phish and Widespread Panic were everywhere, the East Coast festival circuit was thriving, and local spots like The East End Cafe and The Deer Park Tavern were packed every weekend. We played constantly, sometimes twice a week, shared the stage with The Recipe, Schleigho, Lake Trout, The Disco Biscuits, Brothers Past, The Slip, Humonculus, and others, and made it to festivals up and down the East Coast, including Phish's Camp Oswego in 1999. No studio recordings, but some live audio made it:

<iframe width='100%' height='450' scrolling='no' frameborder='no' allow='autoplay' src='https://w.soundcloud.com/player/?url=https%3A//api.soundcloud.com/playlists/2287393&color=%230e1110&auto_play=false&hide_related=false&show_comments=true&show_user=true&show_reposts=false&show_teaser=true'></iframe>
A few members also played a live set on Main St, Newark, DE on September 2, 2000:

<iframe width='100%' height='300' scrolling='no' frameborder='no' allow='autoplay' src='https://w.soundcloud.com/player/?url=https%3A//api.soundcloud.com/playlists/soundcloud%253Aplaylists%253A101787743&color=%23ff5500&auto_play=false&hide_related=false&show_comments=true&show_user=true&show_reposts=false&show_teaser=true&visual=true'></iframe>

music.heading: Yuba Rift
content: Yuba Rift never played many shows and never really tried to. We recorded on a tape deck in the corner of a practice space in Newark, DE, and the recordings sound like it. The quality is rough, but these are some of the most open and exploratory sessions I've been part of. No commercial ambition, just a small group of people seeing where things went.

<iframe width='100%' height='450' scrolling='no' frameborder='no' allow='autoplay' src='https://w.soundcloud.com/player/?url=https%3A//api.soundcloud.com/playlists/soundcloud%253Aplaylists%253A8377240&color=%23ff5500&auto_play=false&hide_related=false&show_comments=true&show_user=true&show_reposts=false&show_teaser=true'></iframe>

music.heading: Omnisoul
content: After the Healthy Doses wrapped up, I started Omnisoul with a tighter focus on original songwriting and radio. We came out of the same UD scene and played the same venues, but the approach was different. We won UD's Battle of the Bands, held down The Stone Balloon, The East End Cafe, and The Deer Park Tavern, and earned support slots with Sugar Ray, Collective Soul, Lifehouse, and the All American Rejects.
Our single "Waiting (Save Your Life)" became a request chart fixture on WSTW 93.7, which eventually got us in front of Wind-up Records.

<iframe height='450' width='100%' title='Media player' src='https://embed.music.apple.com/us/album/happy-outside/1754410766?itscg=30200&itsct=music_box_player&ls=1&app=music&mttnsubad=1754410766&theme=dark' id='embedPlayer' sandbox='allow-forms allow-popups allow-same-origin allow-scripts allow-top-navigation-by-user-activation' allow='autoplay *; encrypted-media *; clipboard-write' style='border: 0px; border-radius: 12px; width: 100%; height: 450px; max-width: 660px;'></iframe>

music.heading: The Crash Motive
content: Omnisoul landed a full deal with Wind-up Records (home to Evanescence, Creed, Seether, Finger Eleven), covering recording, distribution, touring, merchandising, and publishing, and rebranded as The Crash Motive. We recorded Consequence at Avatar Studios (formerly The Power Station) in NYC, which Billboard flagged as a key upcoming release and Drum! Magazine reviewed, praising the band's pop-with-power sensibility.
Placements from the record include:
• Super Bowl XLII (Fox)
• Madden NFL 2007 (EA Sports)
• The Fantastic Four movie and soundtrack (20th Century Fox)
• Licensed for 30+ NFL, MLB, NCAA, and NHL stadiums
• Network TV: NBC, FOX, CBS promotional campaigns; 2008 Olympic Trials; ESPN programming

<iframe height='450' width='100%' title='Media player' src='https://embed.music.apple.com/us/album/consequence/1444007166?itscg=30200&itsct=music_box_player&ls=1&app=music&mttnsubad=1444007166&theme=dark' id='embedPlayer' sandbox='allow-forms allow-popups allow-same-origin allow-scripts allow-top-navigation-by-user-activation' allow='autoplay *; encrypted-media *; clipboard-write' style='border: 0px; border-radius: 12px; width: 100%; height: 450px; max-width: 660px;'></iframe>

music.heading: SpeakerCity
content: After the Crash Motive wound down, I co-founded SpeakerCity with a group of top-tier players from the Philadelphia area. We set out to build the most dynamic event band in the Mid-Atlantic and didn't ease into it. We debuted to over 2,000 people at the University of Delaware's Bob Carpenter Center, which immediately landed us a summer residency at NorthBeach in Dewey Beach.

Over the next six years we covered the regional circuit across the Jersey Shore, Ocean City, Philadelphia, and D.C., playing three-hour sets that moved between modern pop, hip-hop, alternative rock, and classic R&B. It pushed me to develop a stylistic range I still rely on.

music.heading: Studio Sessions with Brad Cox
content: Even while everything else was happening, I kept working with Brad Cox. Both sessions were with producer Tony Correlli at The Deep End Studio in Baltimore.
In 2013, I played bass on "A Night in Hell & A Sunday Morning," released as a Skitzo Calypso single:

<iframe style='border-radius:12px; width:100%;' src='https://open.spotify.com/embed/album/6xyAVtQ5oWnMK3dFWVhiC3?utm_source=generator&theme=0' height='152' frameborder='0' allow='autoplay; clipboard-write; encrypted-media; fullscreen; picture-in-picture' loading='lazy'></iframe>
In February 2020, I played drums on "Nothing Left to Burn," released under Brad's project Veteran Avenue. It landed on SiriusXM.

<iframe allow='autoplay *; encrypted-media *; fullscreen *; clipboard-write' frameborder='0' height='175' style='width:100%;overflow:hidden;background:transparent;border-radius:12px;' sandbox='allow-forms allow-popups allow-same-origin allow-scripts allow-storage-access-by-user-activation allow-top-navigation-by-user-activation' src='https://embed.music.apple.com/us/album/no-reflection-after-sunset-ep/1602773266?i=1602773268'></iframe>

music.heading: Solo Electronic Work
content: Alongside everything else, I've spent about 25 years recording instrumental electronic music on my own. It's all sketches and ideas, nothing built around songs in the traditional sense. No plans to do anything with it, just something I keep coming back to.

<iframe width='100%' height='450' scrolling='no' frameborder='no' allow='autoplay' src='https://w.soundcloud.com/player/?url=https%3A//api.soundcloud.com/playlists/soundcloud%253Aplaylists%253A344619671&color=%23ff5500&auto_play=false&hide_related=false&show_comments=true&show_user=true&show_reposts=false&show_teaser=true'></iframe>
